directives.title: Optimize Agent Prompt Token Efficiency
steps: Analyze current prompt token lengths for all active agents (e.g., Clinical Documentation Agent, Prior Authorization Agent).
Identify redundant phrases, verbose instructions, or unnecessary examples within agent prompts.
Refactor identified prompts to reduce token count while maintaining or improving task quality.
Evaluate the refactored prompts for quality score parity with previous versions.
Deploy optimized prompts to production.
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly states reducing prompt tokens from 1,800 to 1,100 with 'exact same quality score', providing clear before/after states and measurable outcomes for prompt tuning.

directives.title: Reduce Workflow Flowstep Overhead from Incomplete EHR Queries
steps: Analyze workflow logs to identify instances where the initial EHR query returns incomplete data, triggering follow-up steps.
Investigate the root causes of incomplete EHR query responses (e.g., API issues, malformed requests, data latency).
Implement logic to anticipate or pre-process EHR queries to minimize incompleteness.
Refine the workflow orchestration to reduce or consolidate follow-up steps triggered by initial EHR query issues.
Monitor average flow steps post-deployment.
directives.rationale: The chunk explicitly identifies the problem ('initial EHR query comes back incomplete and triggers a follow-up') and quantifies the overhead (1.2 steps), providing a clear focus for optimization efforts to reduce average steps from 7.2 to 6.0.

directives.title: Enhance Clinical Agent Document Retrieval Precision@5
steps: Evaluate the current clinical agent's document retrieval mechanism, specifically focusing on the top five most relevant clinical notes.
Identify cases where retrieved documents are deemed irrelevant to the authorization decision.
Experiment with alternative retrieval algorithms, embedding models, or re-ranking strategies.
Update the clinical agent's retrieval component with the improved mechanism.
Measure and report the new Precision@5 score.
directives.rationale: The chunk states the current Precision@5 (0.84) and explicitly directs to 'work on pushing that even higher', providing a clear, measurable improvement goal for the retrieval system.

directives.title: Build Enhanced Retry Logic for Agent Handoff Failures
steps: Analyze logs for agent-to-agent handoff failures, specifically isolating those caused by 'ehr systems unavailability'.
Design a robust retry mechanism (e.g., with exponential backoff and maximum retry attempts) for inter-agent communication.
Implement the designed retry logic within the agent orchestration or handoff service component.
Simulate EHR system unavailability to thoroughly test the new retry mechanism.
Monitor the handoff success rate after deployment.
directives.rationale: The chunk clearly identifies the failure rate (1.3%), the primary cause ('ehr systems unavailability'), and the concrete solution ('build better retry logic'), making this directive highly actionable and testable.
